turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hi andrea , how is andrea ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: andrea is doing well . how is the doctor ?

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: the doctor is doing well . uh , so the doctor knows the nurse told andrea about dax . the doctor would like to tell dax a little bit about andrea . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so , andrea is a 52-year-old female with a past medical history significant for rheumatoid arthritis , atrial fibrillation , and reflux who presents today for andrea's annual exam . so andrea , it's been a year since the doctor saw andrea . how is andrea doing ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: andrea is doing well . so , andrea has been walking like the doctor told andrea to and , um , exercising and doing yoga , and walking and exercising and doing yoga has actually helped with andrea's arthritis a lot , just the- the constant movement . so , andrea has n't had any joint pain recently .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . good . so , no- no issues with any stiffness or pain or flare ups over the last year ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and the doctor knows that the doctor has andrea on the methotrexate , is andrea still taking methotrexate once a week ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yes , andrea is .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and any issues with methotrexate ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: no . okay . and then in terms of andrea's a-fib , how is andrea doing with atrial fibrillation ? is andrea having any palpitations ? or , the doctor knows that andrea's kind of been in and out of atrial fibrillation over the past , you know , year or so .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah . andrea has still been having palpitations . the- the last palpitation episode andrea had was about a week ago . andrea has noticed that when andrea starts to get stressed , um , the palpitations start to flare up again . so , andrea has been trying meditation , trying running with andrea's dog to try and relieve the stress but meditation and running with andrea's dog has n't really been working .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: yeah . the doctor- the doctor knows that andrea had called , um , last month and the doctor- the doctor did the event monitor for andrea , uh , which the doctor will take a look at in a few minutes . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: um , how about um , andrea's reflux ? you know , the doctor had placed andrea on the protonix , uh , has protonix helped ? and the doctor knows that andrea was gon na do some dietary modifications .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah . andrea cut out soda and cutting out soda- that's helped- seemed to help , and the protonix has been helping too . andrea has n't had a reflux flare up in over , i think , five months .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . um , so , you know , the doctor knows that andrea did the review of systems sheet when andrea checked in and , you know , andrea had- you know , andrea endorsed the palpitations and andrea had some nasal congestion . any other symptoms ? you know , chest pain , shortness of breath , nausea or vomiting ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , nothing like chest pain , shortness of breath , nausea or vomiting . just the nasal- nasal congestion because of andrea's allergies .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . okay . well , the doctor would like to go ahead and do a quick physical exam , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: all right . hey dragon , show the doctor the vital signs . okay . so , andrea's in- here in the office today , it looks like , you know , andrea's heart rate's really good today . andrea's heart rate is- it's nice and controlled so that's good . um , the doctor is just gon na take a look into andrea's heart and lungs and- and the doctor will let andrea know what the doctor finds . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . so , on physical examination , um , you know , everything looks really good . on andrea's heart examination , the doctor does appreciate a slight 2/6 systolic- systolic ejection murmur , um , which the doctor has heard in the past so the doctor is not worried at the slight 2/6 systolic-systolic ejection murmur . andrea's in the- a nice regular rate and rhythm at this time . andrea's lungs are nice and clear . on andrea's right elbow , the doctor does notice some edema and some erythema . does andrea's right elbow hurt when the doctor presses andrea's right elbow ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah , andrea's right elbow does a bit .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . so , andrea has pain to palpation of the right elbow . um , and andrea has no lower extremity edema , okay ? um , so the doctor wan na go ahead and just take a look at some of andrea's results . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey dragon , show the doctor the event- event monitor results . okay . so , you know , the event monitor results show that , you know , andrea's in and out of a-fib , andrea has what the doctor calls a conversion pause . you know , andrea's in a-fib , andrea pauses , and then andrea goes back to regular rhythm . so , the doctor will talk about the conversion pause , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey dragon , show the doctor the autoimmune panel . so , looking here at andrea's autoimmune panel , everything looks good , it looks like andrea's- you know , everything is well controlled with andrea's rheumatoid arthritis on the methotrexate . okay ? so , let the doctor just go over a little bit about the doctor's assessment and the doctor's plan for andrea . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so for andrea's first problem , andrea's rheumatoid arthritis , again , everything looks good . the doctor wan na just continue andrea on the methotrexate 2.5 mg , once weekly . um , and uh , if andrea needs a referral back to see the rheumatologist , let the doctor know , but the doctor thinks everything seems stable now . does andrea need a refill of the methotrexate ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yes , andrea does .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . hey dragon , order methotrexate , 2.5 mg once weekly . for andrea's second problem , the atrial fibrillation . so , andrea's going in and out of a-fib and the doctor would like to just keep andrea in normal sinus rhythm . so , the doctor wan na go ahead and refer andrea to cardiology for a cardiac ablation which just maps out where the atrial fibrillation rhythm is coming from and burns the atrial fibrillation rhythm so the atrial fibrillation rhythm does n't come back . okay ? andrea's young , the doctor wan na keep andrea in a normal rhythm and , being that andrea's going in and out of a-fib , the doctor thinks a cardiac ablation is what the doctor should do . okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey dragon , order a referral to cardiology . and for andrea's last problem , the reflux , you know , the doctor wanna- the doctor want andrea to just continue on the protonix , 40 mg a day . continue with andrea's dietary modifications , you know , avoiding coffee and spicy foods , that type of thing . okay ? and then let the doctor know if andrea has any other issues with reflux , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: andrea will do .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: any questions ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , andrea do n't .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . it was good to see andrea .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: good seeing the doctor .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey dragon , finalize the note .
